title: Per-allele (coding, non-coding) substitution-count histograms at each Class I locus partition into 6 GMM components — three recurring archetypes (near-reference core, tight multi-serogroup lineages, null-enriched heterogeneous) recapitulate known serogroup structure

subject.display: Migration cohort for 'Per-allele (coding, non-coding) substitution-count histograms at each Class I lo'
subject.note: Inclusion predicates intentionally empty at migration time; the implicit member set is defined by the v1.1 premises. Re-author with explicit inclusion predicates to tighten the subject.

source.note: 4-field Class I alleles with both coding and non-coding substitution counts against the *01:01:01:01 reference. HLA-A 5754, HLA-B 6937, HLA-C 6184 alleles.
Per-allele WHO-HLA serogroup (1-field nomenclature group, e.g., A1, A2, B7, C1, C2).

operations.note: Per-locus GMM fit over k=1..6 with full covariance matrices; BIC selects optimal k.
Independent density-based clustering cross-check to confirm the GMM partition is not driven by Gaussian assumption.
Per-cluster composition: n alleles, n distinct serogroups, null-suffix fraction.

statistics.name: gmm_bic_optimal_k_per_locus
statistics.note: BIC selects k=6 at every Class I locus. Clean agreement across the three loci.

statistics.name: hdbscan_k_per_locus_range
statistics.note: HDBSCAN selects 4–5 clusters per locus (ignoring noise points). Close agreement with GMM 6 — the partition is not a Gaussian artifact.

statistics.name: near_reference_core_cluster_count
statistics.note: Each of the 3 Class I loci has exactly one near-reference-core cluster: HLA-A C2 (n=919, 6 serogroups), HLA-B C0 (n=824, 6 serogroups), HLA-C C1 (n=1361, 12 serogroups). Shared archetype #1.

statistics.name: tight_multi_serogroup_cluster_count
statistics.note: Archetype #2: tight multi-serogroup non-coding backbones with unusually small within-cluster SD. HLA-A exemplars: C4 (n=1576, 10 serogroups, NC SD=5.3 ≈ 1% of mean 617), C0 (n=1463, 13 serogroups, NC SD=39.4), C3 (n=481, 2 serogroups, NC SD=29.7).

statistics.name: per_locus_null_enriched_cluster_null_pct
statistics.note: Archetype #3: one null-enriched heterogeneous cluster per locus (10.6–22.7% null-suffix alleles vs 1.8–3.4% baseline at other clusters). Spans the most serogroups (19, 34, 13 respectively) — the structured relaxation-of-selection subpopulation.

statistics.name: baseline_null_pct_at_non_null_clusters
statistics.note: Upper bound of baseline null frequency at non-null-enriched clusters (range 1.8–3.4%). The null-enriched archetype is at least 3× this baseline at every locus.

inference.justification: Two conjuncts license the 'histogram modes recur as 3 archetypes across loci' claim: (1) near-reference core cluster count >= 3 — one such cluster exists at every Class I locus, confirming archetype #1 is a shared structural feature not a locus-specific artifact; (2) baseline null-suffix fraction at non-enriched clusters < 5% — the archetype #3 null-enriched clusters (10.6–22.7%) are meaningfully separated from baseline, so 'heterogeneous null-enriched' is a distinct archetype rather than an artifact of broad sampling. The GMM k=6 and HDBSCAN agreement (4–5) are pinned descriptive stats that support but do not directly gate the inference — the per-locus archetype existence is the load-bearing fact.
inference.failure_mode: If any Class I locus lacked a near-reference core cluster, archetype #1 would dissolve and 'three recurring archetypes' becomes 'two'. If baseline null fractions were 5–10%, the null-enriched archetype's specificity would weaken.

conclusion.assertion: At each of the three Class I HLA loci (A, B, C), per-allele histograms of (coding, non-coding) substitution counts against the *01:01:01:01 reference partition into k=6 GMM components (BIC-optimal), with HDBSCAN agreeing at 4–5 clusters. The 18 resulting clusters across loci organize into three recurring archetypes: (1) one near-reference core cluster per locus (tight, few serogroups); (2) three to four tight multi-serogroup lineages per locus (extreme NC homogeneity across serogroup-diverse alleles, consistent with pre-diversification backbones or gene-conversion-driven concerted evolution); (3) one null-enriched heterogeneous cluster per locus (10.6–22.7% null-suffix vs 1.8–3.4% elsewhere, highest serogroup breadth). The recurrence of these three archetypes is independent of any prior serogroup assignment.

scope.scale_note: Class I only. Class II DRB1/DQB1/DPB1 cluster architecture is deferred — distinct decomposition because the NC/coding fold is 10× different and the null-allele landscape is sparser.

confidence.note: GMM BIC and HDBSCAN agreement are descriptive; per-locus bootstrap of archetype recurrence is not pinned here.

external_assumptions.statement: GMM BIC and HDBSCAN are appropriate clustering methods for 2-D count data with Poisson-like dispersion; k=6 BIC optimum is robust to alternative covariance parameterizations.

Per-allele substitution counts against a single reference (*01:01:01:01) faithfully capture relative allelic divergence for clustering; alternative references would produce similar k=6 archetypes modulo relabeling.

The observation that serogroup assignments were not used in clustering but cluster composition recapitulates serogroup structure is informative — alternative explanation (balancing selection signatures of different modes producing this geometry) cannot be fully excluded.
